summary: Iteration 4 testing focused on shine-fix (duplicate SVG IDs), Overpass Google Font, and whizz-bang (SignalScroll + SignalMark). All 6 backend pytest tests pass (fit endpoint regression). Frontend: unique per-instance SVG IDs verified (4 SVGs, 12 IDs, all unique); shine sweep animation independently plays on hero (Δx=735px, 10 unique positions) and footer wordmark (Δx=217px, 10 unique positions); SignalScroll rail present (left=20px, w=2px), scaleY grows 0→1 as scroll advances, hidden on mobile 375; SignalMark present on all 7 pages with the correct three brand-blue bars (rgb(24,72,135), rgb(66,111,182), rgb(65,173,226)) and pulses to opacity 1. Overpass Google Fonts CSS is loaded and applied to nav-link and .font-overpass elements.
critical_code_review_comments: AnimatedLogo.jsx uses React useId() plus uniquifySvg() to rewrite fixed SVG IDs (glow, shinegrad, wmclip, Layer_2, Layer_1-2) per instance — clean fix, verified unique across all 4 mounted SVGs.

SignalScroll relies on framer-motion useScroll/useSpring; scaleY reads back as matrix(1,0,0,0,0,0) at scrollTop=0 (scaleY=0) and 'none' at bottom (identity) — expected FM optimization, animation is working.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Shine-fix verified at DOM level (unique IDs) AND at animation level (per-SVG shine rect x-position changes independently on hero and footer instances). SignalScroll and SignalMark render correctly across all 7 routes. Backend fit endpoint unchanged and passing.
rca of the issue: Original bug: multiple AnimatedLogo/Wordmark instances shared hardcoded SVG IDs (wmclip/glow/shinegrad). When more than one instance mounted, browsers deduplicate/redirect the url(#id) references to the first-defined element, breaking downstream references. Fix: uniquifySvg() rewrites those IDs with a React useId()-derived suffix per instance.
